mnemonics: RE-SAMPLE: Repeatedly Estimate, Select And Model to Predict Like Experts
keyPoints: Resampling methods like cross-validation and bootstrapping allow for the estimation of test error without a separate validation set.
Cross-validation, especially k-fold, provides a more reliable estimate of model performance than a single train/test split.
The bootstrap method can be used to estimate the variability of a statistic by repeatedly sampling with replacement from the original data.
commonMistakes: Using the same resampling method (e.g., LOOCV) for all models without considering computational cost, especially for large datasets.
Failing to account for data leakage when performing resampling, which leads to overly optimistic performance estimates.
Applying standard k-fold cross-validation to time-series data, which violates the assumption of independent observations.
